The design integrates with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\n6\t\n7\t## Design Goals\n8\t\n9\t1. **Clear Error Categorization**: Distinct error types for different failure modes\n10\t2. **Rich Context Information**: Line numbers, column positions, source snippets\n11\t3. **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Compile-time guarantees with Result<T, ParseError>\n12\t4. **Backward Compatibility**: Integration with existing error types\n13\t5. **Error Propagation Strategy**: Clear patterns for error transformation and chaining\n14\t\n15\t## Error Type Hierarchy\n16\t\n17\t```\n18\tEnhancedParseError (comprehensive error type)\n19\t├── Kind: ParseErrorKind (enum-style discriminator)\n20\t│   ├── Syntax (YAML syntax errors)\n21\t│   ├── Structure (structure errors like duplicate keys)\n22\t│   ├── TypeMismatch (type conversion errors)\n23\t│   ├── IO (file I/O errors)\n24\t│   ├── Validation (semantic validation errors)\n25\t│   ├── Schema (schema validation errors)\n26\t│   ├── Empty (empty file errors)\n27\t│   └── Unknown (unknown error types)\n28\t├── Context: ErrorContext (location and source info)\n29\t│   ├── Line (line number)\n30\t│   ├── Column (column number)\n31\t│   ├── Snippet (source line)\n32\t│   ├── SurroundingLines (context lines)\n33\t│   └── SnippetLineIndex (error line index)\n34\t├── Detail: ParseErrorDetail (error-specific details)\n35\t│   ├── Syntax fields (Expected, Found)\n36\t│   ├── Structure fields (DuplicateKey, Location)\n37\t│   ├── Type fields (FieldPath, ExpectedType, ActualType, Value)\n38\t│   ├── Validation fields (ConstraintType, Constraint)\n39\t│   └── Schema fields (SchemaPath, SchemaName)\n40\t└── Implements YAMLError interface (Code, YAMLErrorType, Context)\n41\t```\n42\t\n43\t## Files Created\n44\t\n45\t1. **parse_error_design.go** - EnhancedParseError implementation with:\n46\t   - ParseErrorKind enum for error categorization\n47\t   - ErrorContext for location and source snippets\n48\t   - ParseErrorDetail for error-specific information\n49\t   - Construction helpers for each error kind\n50\t   - Legacy conversion methods\n51\t   - Kind checker methods\n52\t\n53\t2. **parse_result.go** - Result<T, ParseError> pattern implementation with:\n54\t   - ParseResultWithError[T] generic type\n55\t   - Ok/Err construction functions\n56\t   - Unwrap methods with safety guarantees\n57\t   - Map/AndThen/OrElse chaining operations\n58\t   - Batch operation support\n59\t   - The design integrates with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\n\n## Design Goals\n\n1. **Clear Error Categorization**: Distinct error types for different failure modes\n2. **Rich Context Information**: Line numbers, column positions, source snippets\n3. **Type-Safe Error Handling**: Compile-time guarantees with Result<T, ParseError>\n4. **Backward Compatibility**: Integration with existing error types\n5. **Error Propagation Strategy**: Clear patterns for error transformation and chaining\n\n## Error Type Hierarchy\n\n```\nEnhancedParseError (comprehensive error type)\n├── Kind: ParseErrorKind (enum-style discriminator)\n│   ├── Syntax (YAML syntax errors)\n│   ├── Structure (structure errors like duplicate keys)\n│   ├── TypeMismatch (type conversion errors)\n│   ├── IO (file I/O errors)\n│   ├── Validation (semantic validation errors)\n│   ├── Schema (schema validation errors)\n│   ├── Empty (empty file errors)\n│   └── Unknown (unknown error types)\n├── Context: ErrorContext (location and source info)\n│   ├── Line (line number)\n│   ├── Column (column number)\n│   ├── Snippet (source line)\n│   ├── SurroundingLines (context lines)\n│   └── SnippetLineIndex (error line index)\n├── Detail: ParseErrorDetail (error-specific details)\n│   ├── Syntax fields (Expected, Found)\n│   ├── Structure fields (DuplicateKey, Location)\n│   ├── Type fields (FieldPath, ExpectedType, ActualType, Value)\n│   ├── Validation fields (ConstraintType, Constraint)\n│   └── Schema fields (SchemaPath, SchemaName)\n└── Implements YAMLError interface (Code, YAMLErrorType, Context)\n```\n\n## Files Created\n\n1. **parse_error_design.go** - EnhancedParseError implementation with:\n   - ParseErrorKind enum for error categorization\n   - ErrorContext for location and source snippets\n   - ParseErrorDetail for error-specific information\n   - Construction helpers for each error kind\n   - Legacy conversion methods\n   - Kind checker methods\n\n2. **parse_result.go** - Result<T, ParseError> pattern implementation with:\n   - ParseResultWithError[T] generic type\n   - Ok/Err construction functions\n   - Unwrap methods with safety guarantees\n   - Map/AndThen/OrElse chaining operations\n   - Batch operation support\n   - Type aliases for common use cases\n\n3. **parse_error_design_test.go** - Comprehensive error type tests\n4. **parse_result_test.go** - Comprehensive Result pattern tests\n\n## Summary\n\nThe ParseError design provides comprehensive error handling for YAML parsing with clear categorization, rich context, and type-safe error handling patterns.\n","numLines":67,"startLine":1,"totalLines":67}}}

failure cases.\n2\t//\n3\t// This file implements the comprehensive ParseError design that integrates\n4\t// with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\n5\tpackage yamlutil\n6\t\n7\timport (\n8\t\t\"fmt\"\n9\t\t\"strings\"\n10\t)\n11\t\n12\t// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n13\t// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\n14\ttype ParseErrorKind string\n15\t\n16\tconst (\n17\t\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n18\t\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n19\t\n20\t\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n21\t\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n22\t\n23\t\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n24\t\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type_mismatch\"\n25\t\n26\t\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n27\t\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n28\t\n29\t\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n30\t\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n31\t\n32\t\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n33\t\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n34\t\n35\t\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n36\t\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n37\t\n38\t\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n39\t\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n40\t)\n41\t\n42\t// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n43\t// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\n44\ttype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n45\t\t// Syntax-specific fields\n46\t\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n47\t\tFound    string // What was actually found\n48\t\n49\t\t// Structure-specific fields\n50\t\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n51\t\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n52\t\n53\t\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n54\t\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n55\t\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n56\t\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n57\t\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n58\t\n59\t\t// Validation-specific fields\n60\t\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n61\t\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n62\t\n63\t\t// Schema-specific fields\n64\t\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n65\t\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n66\t}\n67\t\n68\t// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\n69\ttype ErrorContext struct {\n70\t\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n71\t\tLine int\n72\t\n73\t\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n74\t\tColumn int\n75\t\n76\t\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n77\t\tSnippet string\n78\t\n79\t\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n80\t\tSurroundingLines []string\n81\t\n82\t\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n83\t\tSnippetLineIndex int\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n87\t//\n88\t// This enhanced error type includes:\n89\t// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n90\t// - Detailed error-specific information\n91\t// - Rich context including source snippets\n92\t// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\n93\ttype EnhancedParseError struct {\n94\t\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n95\t\tKind ParseErrorKind\n96\t\n97\t\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n98\t\tFilePath string\n99\t\n100\t\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n101\t\tMessage string\n102\t\n103\t\t// Context provides location and source context information\n104\t\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n105\t\n106\t\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n107\t\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n108\t\n109\t\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n110\t\tUnderlyingErr error\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t// Error implements the error interface.\n114\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n115\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n116\t\n117\t\t// Build error header\n118\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n119\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n120\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n121\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n122\t\t\t}\n123\t\t} else {\n124\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n125\t\t}\n126\t\n127\t\t// Add message\n128\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n129\t\n130\t\t// Add kind-specific details\n131\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n132\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n133\t\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n134\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n135\t\t\t}\n136\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n137\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n138\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n139\t\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n140\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n141\t\t\t\t}\n142\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n143\t\t\t}\n144\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n145\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n146\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s\",\n147\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n148\t\t\t}\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n150\t\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n151\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n152\t\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n153\t\t\t}\n154\t\t}\n155\t\n156\t\treturn sb.String()\n157\t}\n158\t\n159\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n160\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n161\t\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n162\t}\n163\t\n164\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\n165\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n166\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n167\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n168\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n169\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n170\t\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n171\t\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n172\t\t\t}\n173\t\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n174\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n175\t\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n176\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n177\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n178\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n179\t\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n180\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n181\t\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n182\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n183\t\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n184\t\tdefault:\n185\t\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n186\t\t}\n187\t}\n188\t\n189\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n190\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n191\t\tswitch e.Kind {\n192\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n193\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n194\t\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n195\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n196\t\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n197\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n198\t\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n199\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n200\t\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n201\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n202\t\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n203\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n204\t\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n205\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n206\t\tdefault:\n207\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n208\t\t}\n209\t}\n210\t\n211\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n212\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n213\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n214\t\n215\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n216\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n217\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n218\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n219\t\t\t}\n220\t\t}\n221\t\n222\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n223\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n224\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n225\t\t\t}\n226\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n230\t\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n231\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n232\t\t\t}\n233\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n234\t\t}\n235\t\n236\t\treturn sb.String()\n237\t}\n238\t\n239\t// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\n240\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n241\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n242\t\n243\t\t// Error header\n244\t\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n245\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n246\t\n247\t\t// Add snippet if available\n248\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n249\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n250\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n251\t\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n252\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n253\t\n254\t\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n255\t\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n256\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n257\t\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n258\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n259\t\t\t\t}\n260\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n261\t\t\t}\n262\t\t}\n263\t\n264\t\t// Add surrounding context if available\n265\t\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n266\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n267\t\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n268\t\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n269\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n270\t\t\t\t} else {\n271\t\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n272\t\t\t\t}\n273\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n274\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n275\t\t\t}\n276\t\t}\n277\t\n278\t\treturn sb.String()\n279\t}\n280\t\n281\t// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\n282\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n283\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n284\t}\n285\t\n286\t// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\n287\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n288\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n289\t}\n290\t\n291\t// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\n292\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n293\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\n297\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n298\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\n302\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n303\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\n307\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n308\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n309\t}\n310\t\n311\t// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\n312\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n313\t\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n314\t}\n315\t\n316\t// ============================================================================\n317\t// Error Construction Helpers\n318\t// ============================================================================\n319\t\n320\t// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\n321\tfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string,    found,\n333\t\t\t},\n334\t\t}\n335\t}\n336\t\n337\t// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\n338\tfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, &EnhancedParseError{\n420\t\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n421\t\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n422\t\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n423\t\t}\n424\t}\n425\t\n426\t// ============================================================================\n427\t// Error Propagation Strategy\n428\t// ============================================================================\n429\t\n430\t// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n431\t//\n432\t// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n433\t//    source snippets whenever possible.\n434\t//\n435\t// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n436\t//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n437\t//\n438\t// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n439\t//    error handling guarantees.\n440\t//\n441\t// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n442\t//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n443\t//\n444\t// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n445\t//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n446\t//\n447\t// Error Flow:\n448\t//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n449\t//\n450\t// Example propagation:\n451\t//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n452\t//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n453\t\n454\t// ============================================================================\n455\t// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n456\t// ============================================================================\n457\t\n458\t// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\n459\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n460\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n461\t\t\treturn nil\n462\t\t}\n463\t\treturn &SyntaxError{\n464\t\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n465\t\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n466\t\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n467\t\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n468\t\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n469\t\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n470\t\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n471\t\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n472\t\t}\n473\t}\n474\t\n475\t// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\n476\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n477\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n478\t\t\treturn nil\n479\t\t}\n480\t\treturn &StructureError{\n481\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n482\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n483\t\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n484\t\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n485\t\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n486\t\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n487\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n488\t\t}\n489\t}\n490\t\n491\t// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\n492\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n493\t\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n494\t\t\treturn nil\n495\t\t}\n496\t\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n497\t\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n498\t\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n499\t\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n500\t\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n501\t\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n502\t\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n503\t\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n504\t\t}\n505\t}\n506\t\n507\t// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\n508\tfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyParseError() *ParseError {\n509\t\treturn &ParseError{\n510\t\t\tFilePath:   e.FilePath,\n511\t\t\tLine:       e.LocationInfo.Line,\n512\t\t\tColumn:     e.LocationInfo.Column,\n513\t\t\tMessage:    e.Message,\n514\t\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n515\t\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n516\t\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n517\t\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n518\t\t}\n519\t}\n520\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ba598ac7-bfae-453e-b689-12f08f70cac6","uuid":"5fd1e6ef-afa2-47ed-9528-74c2cf67aaff","timestamp":"2026-07-11T13:19:43.340Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/parse_error_design.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides enhanced ParseError type design for failure cases.\n//\n// This file implements the comprehensive ParseError design that integrates\n// with the Result<T, ParseError> pattern for type-safe error handling.\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorKind represents the category of parse error.\n// This serves as an enum-style discriminator for different error types.\ntype ParseErrorKind string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorKindSyntax indicates YAML syntax errors (indentation, malformed YAML, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindSyntax ParseErrorKind = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindStructure indicates YAML structure errors (duplicate keys, invalid nesting, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindStructure ParseErrorKind = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch indicates type conversion errors (string to int, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindTypeMismatch ParseErrorKind = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindIO indicates file I/O errors (not found, permission denied, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindIO ParseErrorKind = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindValidation indicates semantic validation errors (required fields, constraints, etc.)\n\tParseErrorKindValidation ParseErrorKind = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindSchema indicates schema validation errors\n\tParseErrorKindSchema ParseErrorKind = \"schema\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindEmpty indicates empty file or content\n\tParseErrorKindEmpty ParseErrorKind = \"empty\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorKindUnknown indicates an unknown error type\n\tParseErrorKindUnknown ParseErrorKind = \"unknown\"\n)\n\n// ParseErrorDetail contains specific details for different error kinds.\n// Only relevant fields are populated based on the ErrorKind.\ntype ParseErrorDetail struct {\n\t// Syntax-specific fields\n\tExpected string // What was expected (for syntax errors)\n\tFound    string // What was actually found\n\n\t// Structure-specific fields\n\tDuplicateKey string // Name of duplicate key (for structure errors)\n\tLocation     string // Nested path to error location\n\n\t// Type mismatch-specific fields\n\tFieldPath    string // Dot-notation path to field (for type errors)\n\tExpectedType string // Expected type\n\tActualType   string // Actual type found\n\tValue        string // Actual value that caused error\n\n\t// Validation-specific fields\n\tConstraintType string // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string // Description of constraint\n\n\t// Schema-specific fields\n\tSchemaPath string // Path to schema file\n\tSchemaName string // Name of schema\n}\n\n// ErrorContext provides rich context about the error location.\ntype ErrorContext struct {\n\t// Line is the line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tLine int\n\n\t// Column is the column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn int\n\n\t// Snippet is the line of source code where error occurred\n\tSnippet string\n\n\t// SurroundingLines provides context around the error line\n\tSurroundingLines []string\n\n\t// SnippetLineIndex is the index of the error line within SurroundingLines\n\tSnippetLineIndex int\n}\n\n// EnhancedParseError provides comprehensive error information for parsing failures.\n//\n// This enhanced error type includes:\n// - Error kind categorization (syntax, structure, type mismatch, etc.)\n// - Detailed error-specific information\n// - Rich context including source snippets\n// - Integration with Result<T, ParseError> pattern\ntype EnhancedParseError struct {\n\t// Kind categorizes the error type\n\tKind ParseErrorKind\n\n\t// FilePath is the path to the file being parsed\n\tFilePath string\n\n\t// Message is a human-readable error message\n\tMessage string\n\n\t// Context provides location and source context information\n\tLocationInfo ErrorContext\n\n\t// Detail contains error-specific details\n\tDetail ParseErrorDetail\n\n\t// UnderlyingErr is the wrapped error for compatibility\n\tUnderlyingErr error\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build error header\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s at line %d\", e.Kind, e.FilePath, e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s error in %s\", e.Kind, e.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", e.Message))\n\n\t// Add kind-specific details\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\tif e.Detail.Expected != \"\" || e.Detail.Found != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (expected: %s, found: %s)\", e.Detail.Expected, e.Detail.Found))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (duplicate key: %s\", e.Detail.DuplicateKey))\n\t\t\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, expected: %s, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.ExpectedType, e.Detail.ActualType))\n\t\t}\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" && e.Detail.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (field: %s, constraint: %s)\",\n\t\t\t\te.Detail.FieldPath, e.Detail.Constraint))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn e.UnderlyingErr\n}\n\n// Code returns the error code for programmatic handling.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\tif e.Detail.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeFileEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\tswitch e.Kind {\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSyntax:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n\tcase ParseErrorKindStructure:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n\tcase ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n\tcase ParseErrorKindIO:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeIO\n\tcase ParseErrorKindValidation:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n\tcase ParseErrorKindSchema:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeSchema\n\tcase ParseErrorKindEmpty:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeEmpty\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeUnknown\n\t}\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) Context() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"line: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Line))\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column: %d\", e.LocationInfo.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s\", e.Detail.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\tif e.Detail.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tif sb.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"location: %s\", e.Detail.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// String returns a detailed multi-line error representation with source snippet.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Error header\n\tsb.WriteString(e.Error())\n\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t// Add snippet if available\n\tif e.LocationInfo.Snippet != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\tsb.WriteString(e.LocationInfo.Snippet)\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\n\t\t// Add column indicator if available\n\t\tif e.LocationInfo.Column > 0 && e.LocationInfo.Column <= len(e.LocationInfo.Snippet) {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < e.LocationInfo.Column-1; i++ {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"^--- here\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Add surrounding context if available\n\tif len(e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines) > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\tfor i, line := range e.LocationInfo.SurroundingLines {\n\t\t\tif i == e.LocationInfo.SnippetLineIndex {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"> \")\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"  \")\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(line)\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsSyntaxError checks if this is a syntax error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSyntaxError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSyntax\n}\n\n// IsStructureError checks if this is a structure error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsStructureError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindStructure\n}\n\n// IsTypeMismatchError checks if this is a type mismatch error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsTypeMismatchError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// IsIOError checks if this is an I/O error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsIOError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindIO\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if this is a validation error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsValidationError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindValidation\n}\n\n// IsSchemaError checks if this is a schema error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsSchemaError() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindSchema\n}\n\n// IsEmpty checks if this is an empty file error.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) IsEmpty() bool {\n\treturn e.Kind == ParseErrorKindEmpty\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Construction Helpers\n// ============================================================================\n\n// NewSyntaxParseError creates a new syntax error.\nfunc NewSyntaxParseError(filePath, message string, line, column int, expected, found string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSyntax,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine:   line,\n\t\t\tColumn: column,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tExpected: expected,\n\t\t\tFound:    found,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureParseError creates a new structure error.\nfunc NewStructureParseError(filePath, message string, line int, duplicateKey, location string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindStructure,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchParseError creates a new type mismatch error.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, expectedType, actualType, value string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewIOParseError creates a new I/O error.\nfunc NewIOParseError(filePath, message string, line int, underlyingErr error) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:          ParseErrorKindIO,\n\t\tFilePath:      filePath,\n\t\tMessage:       message,\n\t\tUnderlyingErr: underlyingErr,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewValidationParseError creates a new validation error.\nfunc NewValidationParseError(filePath, message string, line int, fieldPath, constraintType, constraint string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindValidation,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: ParseErrorDetail{\n\t\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaParseError creates a new schema error.\nfunc NewSchemaParseError(filePath, message string, line int, schemaPath, schemaName string) *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindSchema,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  message,\n\t\tLocationInfo: ErrorContext{\n\t\t\tLine: line,\n\t\t},\n\t\tDetail: *EnhancedParseError {\n\treturn &EnhancedParseError{\n\t\tKind:     ParseErrorKindEmpty,\n\t\tFilePath: filePath,\n\t\tMessage:  \"file is empty or contains only whitespace\",\n\t}\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Error Propagation Strategy\n// ============================================================================\n\n// The error propagation strategy for ParseError follows these principles:\n//\n// 1. Always provide rich context: Include line numbers, column positions, and\n//    source snippets whenever possible.\n//\n// 2. Preserve error chain: Use error wrapping (errors.Wrap, fmt.Errorf with %w)\n//    to maintain the full error chain for debugging.\n//\n// 3. Type-safe error handling: Use Result[T, ParseError] pattern for compile-time\n//    error handling guarantees.\n//\n// 4. Error transformation: Transform lower-level errors (e.g., yaml.v3 errors)\n//    into EnhancedParseError with appropriate context and snippets.\n//\n// 5. Fail-fast on critical errors: I/O errors and syntax errors should immediately\n//    return errors without attempting further processing.\n//\n// Error Flow:\n//   Raw Error → EnhancedParseError (with context) → Result[T, ParseError]\n//\n// Example propagation:\n//   yaml.Unmarshal error → extract line/column → read source snippet →\n//   NewSyntaxParseError with snippet → Result[T, ParseError]\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration with Legacy Error Types\n// ============================================================================\n\n// ToLegacySyntaxError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy SyntaxError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacySyntaxError() *SyntaxError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindSyntax {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:      e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tColumn:    e.LocationInfo.Column,\n\t\tMessage:   e.Message,\n\t\tExpected:  e.Detail.Expected,\n\t\tFound:     e.Detail.Found,\n\t\tErr:       e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode: e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyStructureError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy StructureError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyStructureError() *StructureError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindStructure {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tMessage:      e.Message,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: e.Detail.DuplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     e.Detail.Location,\n\t\tErr:          e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyTypeMismatchError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy TypeMismatchError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) ToLegacyTypeMismatchError() *TypeMismatchError {\n\tif e.Kind != ParseErrorKindTypeMismatch {\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     e.FilePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    e.Detail.FieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: e.Detail.ExpectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   e.Detail.ActualType,\n\t\tValue:        e.Detail.Value,\n\t\tLine:         e.LocationInfo.Line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ToLegacyParseError converts EnhancedParseError to legacy ParseError.\nfunc (e *EnhancedParseError) e.Message,\n\t\tContextStr: e.Context(),\n\t\tErr:        e.UnderlyingErr,\n\t\tErrorType:  e.YAMLErrorType(),\n\t\tErrorCode:  e.Code(),\n\t}\n}\n","numLines":520,"startLine":1,"totalLines":520}}}

a failure with EnhancedParseError.\n//\n// This type enforces error handling at compile time - callers must check the error\n// before accessing data, eliminating nil pointer dereference bugs.\n//\n// Type parameter T is the type of successfully parsed data.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tresult := ParseYAMLToFile[Config](filePath)\n//\tif result.IsError() {\n//\t    log.Printf(\"Parse failed: %v\", result.Error)\n//\t    return\n//\t}\n//\tconfig := result.Unwrap()\ntype ParseResultWithError[T any] struct {\n\t// success indicates whether the operation succeeded\n\tsuccess bool\n\n\t// data contains the successful result (only valid when success is true)\n\tdata T\n\n\t// err contains the parse error (only valid when success is false)\n\terr *EnhancedParseError\n}\n\n// OkParse creates a successful result with data.\nfunc OkParse[T any](data T) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\treturn ParseResultWithError[T]{\n\t\tsuccess: true,\n\t\tdata:    data,\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrParse creates a failed result with an error.\nfunc ErrParse[T any](err *EnhancedParseError) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\treturn ParseResultWithError[T]{\n\t\tsuccess: false,\n\t\terr:     err,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsError returns true if this result represents a failure.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) IsError() bool {\n\treturn !r.success\n}\n\n// IsOk returns true if this result represents success.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) IsOk() bool {\n\treturn r.success\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the successful data.\n// Panics if the result is an error - always check IsOk() first.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Unwrap() T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"called Unwrap on error result: %v\", r.err))\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// UnwrapOr returns the successful data, or the provided default value if error.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) UnwrapOr(defaultValue T) T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn defaultValue\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// UnwrapOrElse returns the successful data, or calls the provided function to\n// compute a default value if error.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) UnwrapOrElse(fn func(*EnhancedParseError) T) T {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn fn(r.err)\n\t}\n\treturn r.data\n}\n\n// Error returns the parse error if this result is an error, nil otherwise.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Error() *EnhancedParseError {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn r.err\n\t}\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// ErrorMsg returns the error message if this result is an error, empty string otherwise.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) ErrorMsg() string {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn r.err.Error()\n\t}\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// Map applies a function to the successful value, transforming the result type.\n// Returns the error unchanged if this result is an error.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) Map(fn func(T) T) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\treturn OkParse(fn(r.data))\n}\n\n// MapErr applies a function to the error, transforming it while preserving success.\n// Returns the success unchanged if this result is successful.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) MapErr(fn func(*EnhancedParseError) *EnhancedParseError) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn ErrParse[T](fn(r.err))\n\t}\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// AndThen chains another operation that returns a Result.\n// If this result is an error, returns the error without calling the function.\n// If this result is successful, calls the function with the data and returns its result.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) AndThen(fn func(T) ParseResultWithError[T]) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn r\n\t}\n\treturn fn(r.data)\n}\n\n// OrElse provides an alternative result if this result is an error.\n// If this result is successful, returns it unchanged.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) OrElse(alternative ParseResultWithError[T]) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn alternative\n\t}\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// OrElseTry provides an alternative computation if this result is an error.\n// If this result is successful, returns it unchanged.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) OrElseTry(fn func() ParseResultWithError[T]) ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn fn()\n\t}\n\treturn r\n}\n\n// String returns a string representation of the result.\nfunc (r ParseResultWithError[T]) String() string {\n\tif r.IsError() {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Err(%v)\", r.err)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Ok(%v)\", r.data)\n}\n\n// successful results\n\tSuccessCount int\n\n\t// ErrorCount is the number of error results\n\tErrorCount int\n}\n\n// CollectParseResults collects a slice of Results into ParseResults with statistics.\nfunc CollectParseResults[T any](results []ParseResultWithError[T]) ParseResults[T] {\n\tsuccessCount := 0\n\terrorCount := 0\n\n\tfor _, r := range results {\n\t\tif r.IsOk() {\n\t\t\tsuccessCount++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\terrorCount++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ParseResults[T]{\n\t\tResults:      results,\n\t\tSuccessCount: successCount,\n\t\tErrorCount:   errorCount,\n\t}\n}\n\n// FilterErrors returns only the error results.\nfunc (pr ParseResults[T]) FilterErrors() []ParseResultWithError[T] {\n\tvar errors []ParseResultWithError[T]\n\tfor _, r := r)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn successes\n}\n\n// ErrorSummary returns a summary of all errors in the results.\nfunc (pr ParseResults[T]) ErrorSummary() string {\n\tif pr.ErrorCount == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No *ValidationError\n239\t\treturn err != nil && (ve != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n240\t}\n241\t\n242\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n243\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n244\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n245\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n246\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n247\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n248\t\t\t\treturn true\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t}\n251\t\treturn false\n252\t}\n253\t\n254\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n255\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n256\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n257\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n258\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n259\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n260\t\t\t\treturn nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n325\t//\n326\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAMLError of the specified type.\n684\tfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n685\t\tif err == nil {\n686\t\t\treturn false\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n690\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n691\t\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n692\t\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType() == errorType\n693\t\t\t}\n694\t\t}\n695\t\treturn false\n696\t}\n697\t\n698\t// IsYAMLError checks if an error is any type of YAMLError.\n699\tfunc IsYAMLError(err error) bool {\n700\t\tif err == nil {\n701\t\t\treturn false\n702\t\t}\n703\t\n704\t\t// Check if error implements YAMLError interface\n705\t\t_, ok := err.(YAMLError)\n706\t\treturn ok\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// GetYAMLErrorType returns the ErrorType of a YAMLError, or ErrorTypeUnknown if not a YAMLError.\n710\tfunc GetYAMLErrorType(err error) ErrorType {\n711\t\tif err == nil {\n712\t\t\treturn \"\"\n713\t\t}\n714\t\n715\t\t// Try ye.YAMLErrorType()\n719\t\t\t}\n720\t\t}\n721\t\n722\t\treturn \"\"\n723\t}\n724\t\n725\t// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n726\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\n727\tfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n728\t\tif err == nil {\n729\t\t\treturn false\n730\t\t}\n731\t\n732\t\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n733\t\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n734\t\t\treturn true\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\t// Check wrapped errors\n738\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n739\t\t\tif os.IsNotExist(unwrapped) {\n740\t\t\t\treturn true\n741\t\t\t}\n742\t\t}\n743\t\n744\t\treturn false\n745\t}\n746\t\n747\t// IsPermissionError checks if an error indicates a permission issue.\n748\t// This works with both FileError and other wrapped permission errors.\n749\tfunc IsPermissionError(err error) bool {\n750\t\tif err == nil {\n751\t\t\treturn false\n752\t\t}\n753\t\n754\t\t// Check wrapped errors\n755\t\tif os.IsPermission(err) {\n756\t\t\treturn true\n757\t\t}\n758\t\n759\t\t// Check if error is FileError with underlying permission error\n760\t\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n761\t\t\tif os.IsPermission(unwrapped) {\n762\t\t\t\treturn true\n763\t\t\t}\n764\t\t}\n765\t\n766\t\treturn false\n767\t}\n768\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"ba598ac7-bfae-453e-b689-12f08f70cac6","uuid":"1c3314e1-dd54-4141-b7a3-97f35e75c01a","timestamp":"2026-07-11T13:19:55.069Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/yamlutil/errors.go","content":"// Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d: %s\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line, pe.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s: %s\", pe.FilePath, pe.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (pe != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath   string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath  string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tMessage    string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine       int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tContextStr string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr        error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType       ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d, field %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at field %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.FieldPath, ve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s: %s\", ve.FilePath, ve.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (ve != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s: %s\", op, fe.Path, fe.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s: %s\", fe.Path, fe.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\nfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n\tvar fe *FileError\n\treturn err != nil && (fe != nil || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n}\n\n// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n//\n// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\ntype SyntaxError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn se.Err\n}\n\n// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n//\n// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\ntype StructureError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where structure error occurred\n\tMessage      string    // Description of the structure error\n\tDuplicateKey string    // Name of duplicate key (if applicable)\n\tLocation     string    // Nested path to the error location\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Context() string {\n\tctx := \"location: \" + ste.Location\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", duplicate key: %s\", ste.DuplicateKey)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Error() string {\n\tif ste.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s at line %d: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Line, ste.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"structure error in %s: %s\", ste.FilePath, ste.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ste.Err\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n//\n// TypeMismatchError occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n// expected Go type (e.g., trying to parse a string as an integer).\ntype TypeMismatchError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with type error\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the field with error\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type description\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found\n\tValue        string    // Actual value that caused the error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif tme.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn tme.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeTypeMismatch\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, expected type: %s, actual type: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\ttme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType, tme.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (tme *TypeMismatchError) Error() string {\n\tif tme.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s at line %d, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.Line, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"type mismatch in %s, field %s: expected %s, got %s\",\n\t\ttme.FilePath, tme.FieldPath, tme.ExpectedType, tme.ActualType)\n}\n\n// FieldNotFoundError represents an error when a required field is missing.\n//\n// FieldNotFoundError is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n// in the YAML data structure.\ntype FieldNotFoundError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file missing the field\n\tFieldPath string    // Dot-notation path to the missing field\n\tLine      int       // Line number where field should be (if known)\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fnfe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fnfe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeRequiredField\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field not found: %s\", fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fnfe *FieldNotFoundError) Error() string {\n\tif fnfe.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s at line %d: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.Line, fnfe.FieldPath)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"required field missing in %s: %s\", fnfe.FilePath, fnfe.FieldPath)\n}\n\n// ConstraintError represents constraint validation failures.\n//\n// ConstraintError is used when a field value violates a defined constraint\n// (e.g., value out of range, string too long, pattern mismatch).\ntype ConstraintError struct {\n\tFilePath       string    // Path to the file with constraint error\n\tFieldPath      string    // Dot-notation path to the field\n\tConstraintType string    // Type of constraint (range, length, pattern, etc.)\n\tConstraint     string    // Description of the constraint\n\tValue          string    // Actual value that violated the constraint\n\tLine           int       // Line number where constraint violation occurred\n\tErrorCode      ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ce.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ce.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeConstraintViolation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"field: %s, constraint: %s, value: %s\",\n\t\tce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint, ce.Value)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ce *ConstraintError) Error() string {\n\tif ce.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s at line %d, field %s: %s\",\n\t\t\tce.FilePath, ce.Line, ce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"constraint violation in %s, field %s: %s\",\n\t\tce.FilePath, ce.FieldPath, ce.Constraint)\n}\n\n// DuplicateKeyError represents an error when duplicate keys are found in YAML.\n//\n// DuplicateKeyError occurs when a YAML mapping contains the same key multiple times,\n// which is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error.\ntype DuplicateKeyError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with duplicate keys\n\tKey       string    // The duplicate key name\n\tLocation  string    // Nested path to the duplicate key\n\tLine1     int       // Line number of first occurrence\n\tLine2     int       // Line number of duplicate occurrence\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif dke.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn dke.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeDuplicateKey\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key: %s\", dke.Key)\n\tif dke.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" at %s\", dke.Location)\n\t}\n\tif dke.Line1 > 0 && dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\" (first at line %d, duplicate at line %d)\", dke.Line1, dke.Line2)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (dke *DuplicateKeyError) Error() string {\n\tif dke.Line2 > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s at line %d: key %q already defined at line %d\",\n\t\t\tdke.FilePath, dke.Line2, dke.Key, dke.Line1)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"duplicate key error in %s: key %q\", dke.FilePath, dke.Key)\n}\n\n// SchemaLoadError represents errors loading schema definitions.\n//\n// SchemaLoadError occurs when a schema file cannot be loaded or parsed.\ntype SchemaLoadError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the load error\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sle.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sle.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaLoad\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Context() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema file: %s\", sle.FilePath)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Error() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema load error in %s: %s\", sle.FilePath, sle.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (sle *SchemaLoadError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn sle.Err\n}\n\n// SchemaValidationError represents schema validation failures.\n//\n// SchemaValidationError occurs when YAML data fails validation against a schema.\ntype SchemaValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath    string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tSchemaPath  string    // Path to the schema file\n\tFieldPath   string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field\n\tMessage     string    // Description of the validation failure\n\tExpected    string    // What was expected by the schema\n\tFound       string    // What was actually found\n\tLine        int       // Line number where validation failed\n\tErrorCode   ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif sve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn sve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeSchemaValidation\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSchemaValidate\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"schema: %s\", sve.SchemaPath)\n\tif sve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", field: %s\", sve.FieldPath)\n\t}\n\tif sve.Expected != \"\" || sve.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", expected: %s, found: %s\", sve.Expected, sve.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (sve *SchemaValidationError) Error() string {\n\tif sve.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema validation error in %s at line %d: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Line, sve.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"schema validation error in %s: %s\", sve.FilePath, sve.Message)\n}\n\n// Helper functions for error type checking\n\n// isYAMLErrorOfType checks if an error is a YAMLError of the specified type.\nfunc isYAMLErrorOfType(err error, errorType ErrorType) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType() == errorType\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsYAMLError checks if an error is any type of YAMLError.\nfunc IsYAMLError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error implements YAMLError interface\n\t_, ok := err.(YAMLError)\n\treturn ok\n}\n\n// GetYAMLErrorType returns the ErrorType of a YAMLError, or ErrorTypeUnknown if not a YAMLError.\nfunc GetYAMLErrorType(err error) ErrorType {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Try to unwrap to get to the base YAMLError\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif ye, ok := unwrapped.(YAMLError); ok {\n\t\t\treturn ye.YAMLErrorType()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn \"\"\n}\n\n// IsFileNotFoundError checks if an error indicates a file was not found.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped file not found errors.\nfunc IsFileNotFoundError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error chain contains os.ErrNotExist\n\tif os.IsNotExist(err) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) {\n\t\tif os.IsNotExist(unwrapped) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// IsPermissionError checks if an error indicates a permission issue.\n// This works with both FileError and other wrapped permission errors.\nfunc IsPermissionError(err error) bool {\n\tif err == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check wrapped errors\n\tif os.IsPermission(err) {\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if error is FileError with underlying permission error\n\tfor unwrapped := err; unwrapped != nil; unwrapped = errors.Unwrap(unwrapped) The task asks me to:\n   - Define ParseError struct with fields (message, line, column, code)\n   - Implement YAMLError interface methods (Code(), Error())\n   - Add constructor function NewParseError\n\nLooking more carefully at the existing `ParseError` struct:\n- It has `Message` (capital M), `Line`, `Column`, and `ErrorCode` (capital EC)\n- The `Code()` method returns `ErrorCode`\n- The `Error()` method returns a formatted string with position context\n\nBut the task description says fields should be: (message, line, column, code) - lowercase. for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// File error codes\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound      ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"      // File does not exist\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\" // Permission denied\n\tErrCodeFileIOError       ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"      // Generic I/O error\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty         ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"         // File is empty\n\n\t// Parse error codes\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax   ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"    // YAML syntax error\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch    ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"    // Type conversion error\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\" // YAML structure error\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey    ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"    // Duplicate mapping key\n\tErrCodeParseError       ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"      // Generic parse error\n\n\t// Validation error codes\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed   ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"    // Validation failed\n\tErrCodeRequiredField      ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"       // Missing required field\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\" // Constraint violated\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue       ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"        // Invalid value\n\n\t// Schema error codes\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"    // Schema loading failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation   ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"    // Schema validation failed\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound     ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"      // Schema not found\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid      ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"         // Invalid schema definition\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred
